title: Israel's long obsession with southern Lebanon and the Litani River
subhead: Israel has intensified its deadly strikes and evacuation orders in Lebanon. The specter of a new invasion haunts the Lebanese population. The country's south, now a stronghold of the pro-Iranian Hezbollah, has preoccupied Israel and its founding fathers for over a century.

title: France's military spending plans face reality of fragile national finances
Macron and Pope Leo XIV call for Middle East dialogue
Iranians express bitterness at war: 'The jailer hasn't changed, my friends, and we are all in line for death'
subhead: In accounts gathered by Le Monde, residents describe enduring record-high inflation and intensified political repression, in addition to the war. The current lull seems fragile and uncertain to them.

title: US consumer inflation surges 3.3% year-on-year amid impact of Iran war
subhead: Gasoline prices surged by 21.2% between February and March – the largest monthly increase since the the government began publishing a gasoline price index in 1967.

title: Ukraine gains upper hand in aerial war against Russia
subhead: Powered by a new generation of drones, Ukrainian forces carried out a record number of strikes in March. Russian oil and gas infrastructure became a primary target, impacting global energy prices.

title: A French judge was found burned at a cliff in Djibouti in 1995. How and why he died remains a mystery
subhead: On October 19, 1995, the body of Bernard Borrel was found burned at the base of a cliff in Djibouti. More than 30 years later, his widow Elisabeth Borrel continues her fight to shed light on the case, which has become entangled in military interests, diplomatic pressure and state secrets.

Denis Charbit: 'The Israeli lawmakers who established the death penalty merely obeyed their own instincts for vengeance'
subhead: The professor at the Open University of Israel looks into the recent law introducing the death penalty for Palestinians convicted of terrorist acts and anti-Israeli attacks. He sees it as a grave moral regression and is concerned about Israel abandoning 'thousands of years of ethics.'

title: AI is reshaping translators' work: 'Translation isn't simply converting words from one language to another'
subhead: New skills are needed, pricing pressure is mounting and 'post-editing' is on the rise. The rise of artificial intelligence is transforming the translation industry and training programs must adapt.

title: Macron to meet Pope Leo XIV on Friday to discuss a world in crisis
subhead: The French president and the American pontiff are expected to address conflicts in Lebanon and Iran and contemporary issues such as the dangers posed by the development of artificial intelligence.
